content_en:
Q. 213. What is the ruling on one who prays in garments that are impure when he does not know them to be so?

A. Ifa person prays in impure garments, and he did not know until after he prayed that they had become soiled by some filth, then the prayer is valid and he does not need to repeat it. This is because he did this forbidden thing unknowingly or forgetfully

'11 Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book: The Penalty for Hunting, in the Chapter: What is Forbidden of Wearing Perfume for the Muhrim and the Muhrimah.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 442 prey | ols,i als

and Allah, the Most Blessed, the Most High says: “Our Lord! Punish us not if we forget or fall into error.’"!}! And He, the Most High says:

“T have done so.”'?!

The Messenger of Allah #% prayed one day in his shoes and there was some filth on them, and during the prayer, Jibril, peace be upon him, came to him and informed him of that. So the Messenger of Allah #¥ took them off during the prayer ?!

and he did not repeat the prayer. This proves that whoever came to know of some impurities during the prayer, he may remove it, even though it is during the prayer. He should continue in his prayer, if it is possible for his ‘Awrah to remain covered after he removes it. Likewise, whoever forgot and then remembered during the prayer, he should remove this soiled garment, if his ‘Awrah remains covered. However, if he completed his prayer and then remembered after being finished, or he came to know of it after he had finished, he is not obliged to repeat it and his prayer is valid, as opposed to a man who prayed and he forgot to perform ablution. For example, if he had broken wind and then forgotten to perform the ablution, then prayed and then remembered after completing the prayer that he had not made ablution in that case, he must perform ablution and repeat the prayer. Likewise, if he was in a state of major ritual impurity (Janabah) and he did not know of it. Like one who had a

'2] Reported by Muslim in the Book of Faith, in the Chapter: Clarification That He, Most Glorified Does not Burden a Person Beyond His Ability.

'5] Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: Prayer Wearing Shoes.

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 443 Beall ghd

nocturnal emission and offered the moming prayer without making Ghusl due to ignorance of the fact on his part, then during the day, he noticed Mani on his garment in that case, he must perform Ghusl and repeat what he had prayed.

The difference between this matter and the first matter I mean the matter of Najasah'"' is that Najasah belongs to the category of leaving what is forbidden, while ablution (Wudu’) and Ghusl belong to the category of doing what is commanded. Doing what is commanded is something compulsory, something that a person must undertake and the act of worship is not complete without it. But as for removing filth, it is a matter of something which should be absent (i.e. the filth) and the prayer is not complete unless it is absent, so if it is present during the prayer during to forgetfulness or ignorance, then it does not harm, because no requisite of the prayer has been left out. And Allah knows better.

Q. 214. What is the punishment for allowing one’s garment to hang below the ankles if one intended to display arrogance, and what is the punishment of it if one did not intend to display pride and arrogance by doing so? How should we respond to one who cites as evidence the Hadith of Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him?

A. If one intends to display pride and arrogance by letting his garment hang down below the ankles, then the punishment is that Allah, the Most High will not look at him on the Day of Resurrection, nor will He speak to him, nor will He absolve him and for him there will be a painful punishment.

- As for one who did not intend to display arrogance, his punishment is that what hangs down below the ankles will be punished in the Fire, because the Prophet # said:

") Najasah: Impurity, filth.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 444 Dey! Olsyh wats

wash V5 chet) She Va tata Gy Gt IR Y aisdy al 5 dts hath saat Sie “There are three persons whom Allah will not look at, nor will He speak to them, nor will He absolve them and for them is a painful punishment: The one whose garment hangs down, the one who performs good deeds and mentions them frequently and the one who swears falsely in order to sell his wares.""") The Prophet 3% said: wil gy J) a bs Joc eg iw “Whoever drags his garment on the ground out of pride and

arrogance, Allah will not look at him on the Day of Resurrection. "71

This is about the one who drags his garment out of pride and arrogance.

As for one who does not intend to display pride and arrogance, it is reported in Sahih Al-Bukhari on the authority of Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with him, that the Prophet #% said:

“Whatever hangs down below the ankles from the Izar will be in the Fire."

'2] Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: Whoever Dragged His Garment on The Ground Due to Pride and Arrogance; and by Muslim in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: The Forbiddance of Dragging One’s Garment on the Ground Due to Pride and Arrogance.

') Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: Whatever Hangs Down Below the Ankles is in the Fire.

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 445

He did not link it to pride and arrogance, nor is it correct to link it to them, according to the Hadith before it, because Abu Sa’id Al-Khudri, may Allah be pleased with him, said: The Messenger of Allah 2% said:

“ath ay ax i} a bs oo Ibs 2515) SE ae “The Izar of the Deliner is at the middle of his shin, and there is no sin (or he said: it is not a sin on him) if it falls anywhere between there and the ankles. And whatever is below that is in the Fire; and whoever drags his Izar on the ground out of pride, Allah will not look at him on the Day of Resurrection." This was narrated by Malik, Abu Dawud, An-Nasa’i, Ibn Majah and Ibn Hibban in his Sahih and it was mentioned in At-Targhib wat-Tarhib in: The Recommendation of Wearing a Qamis, page 88, vol. 3. The two acts are different, and the two punishments are different; and whenever the ruling and the cause differ, it is not possible to link the unconditional to the conditional, since this would necessitate a contradiction.

As for one who cites the Hadith of Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, as evidence against us, we say to him: There is no evidence in it for you for two reasons:

1. That Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, said: “One side of my Izar hangs low if I do not take care of it.”"?!

"I Reported by Imam Ahmad 3:5; and by Abu Dawud in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: Estimating the Place of the Izar (4093); and by Ibn Majah in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: The Place of the Izar, Where is it? (3573); and by An-Nasa’i in the Book of Adornment, in the Chapter: The Place of the Jzar and by Malik (2:217).

"21 Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: Whoever Dragged His Izar Without it Being Due to Pride and Arrogance.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 446 PLY ots yT Gots

But Abu Bakr did not make his Izar hang down by his own choice. Rather, it would hang down itself, and in spite of this, he took care of it. Those who claim that they do not intend to display pride and arrogance by doing so, intentionally make their garments hang down, so we say to them: If you intended to lower your garments below the ankles without intending to display pride and arrogance, you will be punished for what hangs below the ankles in the Fire, and if you dragged your garments on the ground due to pride and arrogance, you will be punished for something greater than that: Allah will not speak to you on the Day of Resurrection, nor will He look at you, nor will he absolve you and you will have a painful punishment.

2. That the Prophet #% absolved Abu Bakr, and testified that he is not one of those who do that out of pride and arrogance. Has any of those people been given this absolution and testimony?! But Satan makes a way for some people following the doubtful things, in the evidences of the Book (of Allah) and the Sunnah, in order to exonerate themselves of what they did, and Allah guides whom He wills to the Straight Path. We ask Allah that He guide and pardon us all. Written on 29/6/1399 A. H.

Q. 215. If a person prayed and then it became clear that he had made Hadath which necessitated Ghusl (what should he do)?

A. Every person who prays and then finds after the prayer that he has major or minor Hadath then it is incumbent upon him to purify himself from this Hadath and to repeat the prayer, because the Prophet # said:

“Allah does not accept prayer without purification.”"")

"1 Reported by Muslim in the Book of Purification, in the Chapter: The Obligation to Purify Oneself for Prayer.

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 447 Beal es ghd

Q. 216. If a person had a nosebleed while he was praying, what is the ruling? Does it make the clothing impure?

A. A nosebleed does not nullify the ablution, whether it is much or little. Likewise, everything that is emitted from the body — besides what comes from the two Sabils'!! - does not nullify ablution, like vomit and pus. They also do not invalidate ablution, whether it be much or little, because this has not been confirmed from the Prophet #2, and the basic principle is that one remains in a state of ritual purity, because this state of purity has been confirmed by legal evidence. And whatever was confirmed by legal evidence cannot be removed except by another legal evidence. There is no evidence that a bodily emission from other than the two Sabils nullifies ablution. So based upon this, ablution is not invalidated by a nosebleed, or by vomiting, whether it be much or little. But if it annoys you during your prayer and you are unable to complete it with humility, then there is no sin upon you if you leave the prayer at that time. Likewise, if you feared that you will soil the Masjid with this blood pouring out, then you must leave so that you do not soil the Masjid. As for the blood which spills on the clothing, it does not make it impure.
content_id:
T. 213. Apa hukum orang yang salat dengan pakaian yang bernajis padahal dia tidak mengetahuinya?

J. Jika seseorang salat dengan pakaian yang bernajis, dan dia tidak mengetahui sampai setelah selesai salat bahwa pakaiannya telah terkena najis, maka salatnya sah dan dia tidak perlu mengulanginya. Hal ini karena dia melakukan hal yang dilarang ini tanpa mengetahuinya atau karena lupa.

'11 Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Denda Berburu, pada Bab: Apa yang Dilarang Memakai Wewangian bagi Muhrim Laki-laki dan Muhrimah.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 442 prey | ols,i als

dan Allah Yang Maha Memberkahi lagi Maha Tinggi berfirman: "Ya Tuhan kami, janganlah Engkau hukum kami jika kami lupa atau kami tersalah."'!} Dan Dia Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

"Sungguh Aku telah melakukannya."'?!

Rasulullah ﷺ suatu hari salat dengan mengenakan sandalnya dan terdapat najis padanya, lalu selama salat, Jibril 'alaihissalam datang kepadanya dan memberitahukan hal tersebut. Maka Rasulullah ﷺ melepas sandalnya selama salat berlangsung'?!

dan beliau tidak mengulangi salatnya. Ini membuktikan bahwa barangsiapa mengetahui adanya najis selama salat, dia boleh menghilangkannya meskipun sedang dalam salat. Dia harus melanjutkan salatnya jika auratnya tetap tertutup setelah dia melepasnya. Demikian pula barangsiapa lupa lalu teringat selama salat, dia harus melepas pakaian yang bernajis tersebut jika auratnya tetap tertutup. Namun jika dia menyelesaikan salatnya lalu baru teringat setelah selesai, atau dia baru mengetahuinya setelah selesai, dia tidak wajib mengulanginya dan salatnya sah, berbeda dengan orang yang salat dan dia lupa berwudu. Misalnya, jika dia kentut lalu lupa berwudu, kemudian salat lalu teringat setelah menyelesaikan salatnya bahwa dia belum berwudu, dalam hal ini dia harus berwudu dan mengulangi salatnya. Demikian pula jika dia dalam keadaan junub dan dia tidak mengetahuinya. Seperti orang yang mengalami

'2] Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Iman, pada Bab: Penjelasan Bahwa Dia Yang Maha Suci Tidak Membebani Seseorang di Luar Kemampuannya.

'5] Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Salat, pada Bab: Salat Memakai Sandal.

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 443 Beall ghd

mimpi basah dan melaksanakan salat subuh tanpa mandi junub karena ketidaktahuannya, lalu pada siang harinya dia menemukan mani pada pakaiannya, dalam hal ini dia harus mandi junub dan mengulangi salat yang telah dia laksanakan.

Perbedaan antara masalah ini dan masalah pertama — yaitu masalah najis'"' — adalah bahwa najis termasuk dalam kategori meninggalkan yang dilarang, sedangkan wudu dan mandi junub termasuk dalam kategori melaksanakan yang diperintahkan. Melaksanakan yang diperintahkan adalah sesuatu yang wajib, sesuatu yang harus dilakukan oleh seseorang dan ibadah tidak sempurna tanpanya. Adapun menghilangkan najis, itu adalah masalah sesuatu yang seharusnya tidak ada (yaitu najis) dan salat tidak sempurna kecuali jika najis tersebut tidak ada. Jika najis itu ada selama salat karena lupa atau ketidaktahuan, maka hal itu tidak membahayakan, karena tidak ada syarat salat yang ditinggalkan. Wallahu a'lam.

T. 214. Apa hukuman bagi orang yang membiarkan pakaiannya menjulur di bawah mata kaki jika dia bermaksud menunjukkan kesombongan, dan apa hukumannya jika dia tidak bermaksud menunjukkan keangkuhan dan kesombongan dengan melakukannya? Bagaimana kita menanggapi orang yang mengutip hadith Abu Bakr radhiyallahu 'anhu sebagai dalil?

J. Jika seseorang bermaksud menunjukkan keangkuhan dan kesombongan dengan membiarkan pakaiannya menjulur di bawah mata kaki, maka hukumannya adalah Allah Yang Maha Tinggi tidak akan memandangnya pada Hari Kiamat, tidak akan berbicara kepadanya, tidak akan menyucikannya, dan baginya azab yang pedih.

- Adapun orang yang tidak bermaksud menunjukkan kesombongan, hukumannya adalah bagian yang menjulur di bawah mata kaki akan diazab dalam Neraka, karena Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

") Najasah: Kotoran, najis.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 444 Dey! Olsyh wats

wash V5 chet) She Va tata Gy Gt IR Y aisdy al 5 dts hath saat Sie "Ada tiga orang yang Allah tidak akan memandang mereka, tidak akan berbicara kepada mereka, dan tidak akan menyucikan mereka, serta bagi mereka azab yang pedih: Orang yang pakaiannya menjulur ke bawah, orang yang melakukan amal kebaikan lalu sering menyebut-nyebutnya, dan orang yang bersumpah palsu untuk menjual dagangannya.""") Nabi ﷺ bersabda: wil gy J) a bs Joc eg iw "Barangsiapa menyeret pakaiannya di tanah karena keangkuhan dan

kesombongan, Allah tidak akan memandangnya pada Hari Kiamat."'71

Ini tentang orang yang menyeret pakaiannya karena keangkuhan dan kesombongan.

Adapun orang yang tidak bermaksud menunjukkan keangkuhan dan kesombongan, diriwayatkan dalam Sahih Al-Bukhari dari Abu Hurairah radhiyallahu 'anhu bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Bagian izar yang menjulur di bawah mata kaki akan berada di Neraka."

'2] Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Pakaian, pada Bab: Barangsiapa Menyeret Pakaiannya di Tanah Karena Keangkuhan dan Kesombongan; dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Pakaian, pada Bab: Larangan Menyeret Pakaian di Tanah Karena Keangkuhan dan Kesombongan.

') Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Pakaian, pada Bab: Bagian yang Menjulur di Bawah Mata Kaki Berada di Neraka.

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 445

Beliau tidak mengaitkannya dengan keangkuhan dan kesombongan, dan tidak benar mengaitkannya dengan keduanya, berdasarkan hadith sebelumnya, karena Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda:

"ath ay ax i} a bs oo Ibs 2515) SE ae "Izar seorang mukmin adalah sampai pertengahan betisnya, dan tidak ada dosa (atau beliau berkata: itu bukan dosa) jika izar itu jatuh di antara situ sampai mata kaki. Dan apa yang di bawah itu berada di Neraka; dan barangsiapa menyeret izarnya di tanah karena kesombongan, Allah tidak akan memandangnya pada Hari Kiamat." Ini diriwayatkan oleh Malik, Abu Dawud, An-Nasa'i, Ibnu Majah, dan Ibnu Hibban dalam Sahih-nya, dan disebutkan dalam At-Targhib wat-Tarhib dalam: Anjuran Memakai Qamis, halaman 88, jilid 3. Kedua perbuatan tersebut berbeda, dan kedua hukumannya berbeda; dan kapan pun hukum dan sebabnya berbeda, tidak mungkin mengaitkan yang mutlak dengan yang bersyarat, karena hal itu akan menimbulkan kontradiksi.

Adapun orang yang mengutip hadith Abu Bakr radhiyallahu 'anhu sebagai dalil terhadap kami, kami katakan kepadanya: Tidak ada dalil di dalamnya untukmu karena dua alasan:

1. Bahwa Abu Bakr radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata: "Salah satu sisi izarku melorot jika aku tidak menjaganya.""?!

"I Diriwayatkan oleh Imam Ahmad 3:5; dan oleh Abu Dawud dalam Kitab Pakaian, pada Bab: Memperkirakan Tempat Izar (4093); dan oleh Ibnu Majah dalam Kitab Pakaian, pada Bab: Tempat Izar, Di Mana Itu? (3573); dan oleh An-Nasa'i dalam Kitab Perhiasan, pada Bab: Tempat Izar dan oleh Malik (2:217).

"21 Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Pakaian, pada Bab: Barangsiapa Menyeret Izarnya Bukan Karena Kesombongan.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 446 PLY ots yT Gots

Tetapi Abu Bakr tidak membuat izarnya menjulur dengan pilihannya sendiri. Sebaliknya, izarnya melorot dengan sendirinya, dan meskipun demikian, beliau tetap menjaganya. Mereka yang mengklaim bahwa mereka tidak bermaksud menunjukkan keangkuhan dan kesombongan dengan melakukannya, secara sengaja membuat pakaian mereka menjulur ke bawah. Maka kami katakan kepada mereka: Jika kalian bermaksud menurunkan pakaian kalian di bawah mata kaki tanpa bermaksud menunjukkan keangkuhan dan kesombongan, kalian akan dihukum atas bagian yang menjulur di bawah mata kaki di Neraka. Dan jika kalian menyeret pakaian kalian di tanah karena keangkuhan dan kesombongan, kalian akan dihukum dengan sesuatu yang lebih besar dari itu: Allah tidak akan berbicara kepada kalian pada Hari Kiamat, tidak akan memandang kalian, tidak akan menyucikan kalian, dan kalian akan mendapat azab yang pedih.

2. Bahwa Nabi ﷺ membebaskan Abu Bakr dari hal tersebut dan bersaksi bahwa beliau bukan termasuk orang-orang yang melakukan hal itu karena keangkuhan dan kesombongan. Apakah ada di antara orang-orang itu yang mendapatkan pembebasan dan kesaksian ini?! Tetapi setan membuat jalan bagi sebagian orang melalui hal-hal yang meragukan dalam dalil-dalil Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah, agar mereka membebaskan diri dari apa yang mereka lakukan. Dan Allah memberi petunjuk kepada siapa yang Dia kehendaki ke jalan yang lurus. Kami memohon kepada Allah agar memberi petunjuk dan memaafkan kita semua. Ditulis pada 29/6/1399 H.

T. 215. Jika seseorang telah salat kemudian diketahui bahwa ia memiliki hadats yang mewajibkan mandi (apa yang harus dilakukan)?

J. Setiap orang yang salat kemudian mengetahui setelah salat bahwa ia memiliki hadats besar atau kecil, maka wajib baginya untuk bersuci dari hadats tersebut dan mengulangi salatnya, karena Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Allah tidak menerima salat tanpa bersuci."

Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Thaharah, pada Bab: Kewajiban Bersuci untuk Salat.

Fatwa-Fatwa Berkaitan dengan Salat

T. 216. Jika seseorang mengalami mimisan saat sedang salat, apa hukumnya? Apakah hal itu menjadikan pakaian najis?

J. Mimisan tidak membatalkan wudhu, baik banyak maupun sedikit. Demikian pula segala sesuatu yang keluar dari tubuh — selain yang keluar dari dua jalan (qubul dan dubur) — tidak membatalkan wudhu, seperti muntah dan nanah. Semua itu juga tidak membatalkan wudhu, baik banyak maupun sedikit, karena hal tersebut tidak terkonfirmasi dari Nabi ﷺ, dan prinsip dasarnya adalah seseorang tetap berada dalam keadaan suci, karena keadaan suci ini telah dikonfirmasi oleh dalil syar'i. Dan apa pun yang telah dikonfirmasi oleh dalil syar'i tidak dapat dihilangkan kecuali dengan dalil syar'i lainnya. Tidak ada dalil bahwa keluarnya sesuatu dari selain dua jalan membatalkan wudhu. Maka berdasarkan hal ini, wudhu tidak batal karena mimisan, atau karena muntah, baik banyak maupun sedikit. Namun jika hal itu mengganggu anda saat salat dan anda tidak mampu menyelesaikannya dengan khusyuk, maka tidak ada dosa atas anda jika meninggalkan salat pada saat itu. Demikian pula, jika anda khawatir akan mengotori masjid dengan darah yang mengalir ini, maka anda harus keluar agar tidak mengotori masjid. Adapun darah yang mengenai pakaian, maka hal itu tidak menjadikannya najis.

Punish us not if we forget or fall into error.’"!}! So رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم #¥ took them off during the الصلاة ?! and he did not repeat the الصلاة. Likewise, whoever forgot and then remembered during the الصلاة, he should remove this soiled garment, if his ‘Awrah remains covered. However, if he completed his الصلاة and then remembered after being finished, or he came to know of it after he had finished, he is not obliged to repeat it and his الصلاة is valid, as opposed to a man who prayed and he forgot to perform ablution. For example, if he had broken wind and then forgotten to perform the ablution, then prayed and then remembered after completing the الصلاة that he had not made ablution in that case, he must perform ablution and repeat the الصلاة. Likewise, if he was in a state of major ritual impurity (Janabah) and he did not know of it. Like one who had a '2] Reported by المسلم in the Book of الإيمان, in the Chapter: Clarification That He, Most Glorified Does not Burden a Person Beyond His Ability. '5] Reported by البخاري in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter: الصلاة Wearing Shoes. الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 443 Beall ghd nocturnal emission and offered the moming الصلاة without making الغسل due to ignorance of the fact on his part, then during the day, he noticed Mani on his garment in that case, he must perform الغسل and repeat what he had prayed. The difference between this matter and the first matter I mean the matter of Najasah'"' is that Najasah belongs to the category of leaving what is محرم, while ablution (الوضوء’) and الغسل belong to the category of doing what is commanded. Doing what is commanded is something واجب, something that a person must undertake and the act of worship is not complete without it. But as for removing filth, it is a matter of something which should be absent (i.e. the filth) and the الصلاة is not complete unless it is absent, so if it is present during the الصلاة during to forgetfulness or ignorance, then it does not harm, because no requisite of the الصلاة has been left out. And الله knows better. Q. 214. What is the punishment for allowing one’s garment to hang below the ankles if one intended to display arrogance, and what is the punishment of it if one did not intend to display pride and arrogance by doing so? How should we respond to one who cites as evidence الحديث النبوي الشريف of Abu Bakr, رضي الله عنه? A. If one intends to display pride and arrogance by letting his garment hang down below the ankles, then the punishment is that الله, the Most High will not look at him on the يوم القيامة, nor will He speak to him, nor will He absolve him and for him there will be a painful punishment. - As for one who did not intend to display arrogance, his punishment is that what hangs down below the ankles will be punished in the Fire, because النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # said: ") Najasah: Impurity, filth. الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 444 Dey! Olsyh wats wash V5 chet) She Va tata Gy Gt IR Y aisdy al 5 dts hath saat Sie “There are three persons whom الله will not look at, nor will He speak to them, nor will He absolve them and for them is a painful punishment: The one whose garment hangs down, the one who performs الأعمال الصالحة and mentions them frequently and the one who swears falsely in order to sell his wares.""") النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم 3% said: wil gy J) a bs Joc eg iw “Whoever drags his garment on the ground out of pride and arrogance, الله will not look at him on the يوم القيامة. "71 This is about the one who drags his garment out of pride and arrogance. As for one who does not intend to display pride and arrogance, it is reported in Sahih البخاري on the authority of أبو هريرة رضي الله عنه, رضي الله عنه, that النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% said: “Whatever hangs down below the ankles from the Izar will be in the Fire." '2] Reported by البخاري in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: Whoever Dragged His Garment on The Ground Due to Pride and Arrogance; and by المسلم in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: The Forbiddance of Dragging One’s Garment on the Ground Due to Pride and Arrogance. ') Reported by البخاري in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: Whatever Hangs Down Below the Ankles is in the Fire. الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 445 He did not link it to pride and arrogance, nor is it correct to link it to them, according to الحديث النبوي الشريف before it, because Abu Sa’id Al-Khudri, رضي الله عنه, said: رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم 2% said: “ath ay ax i} a bs oo Ibs 2515) SE ae “The Izar of the Deliner is at the middle of his shin, and there is no sin (or he said: it is not a sin on him) if it falls anywhere between there and the ankles. And whatever is below that is in the Fire; and whoever drags his Izar on the ground out of pride, الله will not look at him on the يوم القيامة." This was روى by Malik, أبو داود, An-Nasa’i, ابن ماجه and Ibn Hibban in his Sahih and it was mentioned in At-Targhib wat-Tarhib in: The Recommendation of Wearing a Qamis, page 88, vol. 3. The two acts are different, and the two punishments are different; and whenever the ruling and the cause differ, it is not possible to link the unconditional to the conditional, since this would necessitate a contradiction. As for one who cites الحديث النبوي الشريف of Abu Bakr, رضي الله عنه, as evidence against us, we say to him: There is no evidence in it for you for two reasons: 1. That Abu Bakr, رضي الله عنه, said: “One side of my Izar hangs low if I do not take care of it.”"?! "I Reported by الإمام Ahmad 3:5; and by أبو داود in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: Estimating the Place of the Izar (4093); and by ابن ماجه in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: The Place of the Izar, Where is it? (3573); and by An-Nasa’i in the Book of Adornment, in the Chapter: The Place of the Jzar and by Malik (2:217). "21 Reported by البخاري in the Book of Clothing, in the Chapter: Whoever Dragged His Izar Without it Being Due to Pride and Arrogance. الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 446 PLY ots yT Gots But Abu Bakr did not make his Izar hang down by his own choice. Rather, it would hang down itself, and in spite of this, he took care of it. Those who claim that they do not intend to display pride and arrogance by doing so, intentionally make their garments hang down, so we say to them: If you intended to lower your garments below the ankles without intending to display pride and arrogance, you will be punished for what hangs below the ankles in the Fire, and if you dragged your garments on the ground due to pride and arrogance, you will be punished for something greater than that: الله will not speak to you on the يوم القيامة, nor will He look at you, nor will he absolve you and you will have a painful punishment. 2. That النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% absolved Abu Bakr, and testified that he is not one of those who do that out of pride and arrogance. Has any of those people been given this absolution and testimony?! But Satan makes a way for some people following the doubtful things, in the evidences of the Book (of الله) and السنة النبوية, in order to exonerate themselves of what they did, and الله guides whom He wills to the Straight Path. We ask الله that He guide and pardon us all. Written on 29/6/1399 A. H. Q. 215. If a person prayed and then it became clear that he had made Hadath which necessitated الغسل (what should he do)? A. Every person who prays and then finds after the الصلاة that he has major or minor Hadath then it is incumbent upon him to purify himself from this Hadath and to repeat the الصلاة, because النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # said: “الله does not accept الصلاة without purification.”"") "1 Reported by المسلم in the Book of Purification, in the Chapter: The Obligation to Purify Oneself for الصلاة. الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 447 Beal es ghd Q. 216. If a person had a nosebleed while he was praying, what is the ruling? Does it make the clothing impure? A. A nosebleed does not nullify the ablution, whether it is much or little. Likewise, everything that is emitted from the body — besides what comes from the two Sabils'!! - does not nullify ablution, like vomit and pus. They also do not invalidate ablution, whether it be much or little, because this has not been confirmed from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #2, and the basic principle is that one remains in a state of ritual purity, because this state of purity has been confirmed by legal evidence. And whatever was confirmed by legal evidence cannot be removed except by another legal evidence. There is no evidence that a bodily emission from other than the two Sabils nullifies ablution. So based upon this, ablution is not invalidated by a nosebleed, or by vomiting, whether it be much or little. But if it annoys you during your الصلاة and you are unable to complete it with humility, then there is no sin upon you if you leave the الصلاة at that time. Likewise, if you feared that you will soil the المسجد with this blood pouring out, then you must leave so that you do not soil the المسجد. As for the blood which spills on the clothing, it does not make it impure.
